item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the beginning, but the logs are meh. The drone log is fine, but the MTF exploration is really unnecessary, and I think you could take that out and add more hints about it being a living organism in the drone logs?

+1 because there's a lot of good here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came to make exactly this comment. Pedantic readers ftw.

If the author wants it to hunt both birds and mammals, it could be changed to targeting only warm-blooded animals (endothermic animals).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah I'm split on this one. I get a real classic vibe from the article as-is but I feel like the expedition logs tell you too much yknow? Like Without the logs there's a bit more left to the imagination.

I'm gonna +1 regardless since the article I'd like is still here if I just don't open the collaspibles lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both logs were a little bit confusing to follow, especially the end of the second log. The drone took 5 hours to get to the kitchen, but when sci-2 and -4 are escaping, they seem to teleport from the kitchen back to the main building. While yes, on a second read, the timestamps make it a little more clear that a lot of time has passed, it's never clearly indicated that they ran back up the tunnel besides the dialogue suggesting they may have. Still, the story is very good, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sci-3: I'm never going to a Burger King again, Christ. Big Mac all the fucking way now.

yeah me too bud.

I can't honestly give this one an upvote. More just kinda gross than anything else, which I don't think is what was being gone for (and even if it was I'd find it hard to upvote on grossout factor).

Last picture is a nice touch though.

item.title: Location
item.content:encoded: Where is this Burger King in real life

item.title: Very meh -0, +0.
item.content:encoded: Meh. It was ok, but not worth an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is clunky and a little fumbling, but shows potential. The log was a problem for me too, and I think it could be improved substantially of you trusted your audience more. We are smart enough to infer what giant burger with three inactive body cameras means. Do more implying and less imploding telling.

Upvote for potential and to see more from this author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Felt rather forced, as if it was trying to be funny, creepy and generally smart at the same time, while failing to do any of them effectively.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a lot of clunky wording which eventually made the story hard to follow. I enjoyed the first half with the creepy imagery, but the awkward phrasings killed it for me before even getting to the logs. No-vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The latter.

more specifically
Shblammo

It’s a massive larva. The digestive tract is obvious. Each ingredient behaves like a member of the immune system. One of them was even killed by a tongue and uvula. The sign has its proboscis, and the windows that are shaped like hexagons are its eyes. It’s disguising itself as a restaurant to lure prey. And judging by the fact the population of the town is “former,” it’s done it’s job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fridge moment: the existence of an anomalous restaurant suggests the existence of anomalous patrons. Who's been ordering and eating the food?

Alternatively, the restaurant is the disguise of an entity that does the eating, and its waste products are eliminated through the Whopper Popper ™. I'm not sure which is more disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't say I'm a fan of the whopper. It's a double dose of "predatory inanimate thing" and the exploration log is a bit clunky. Worthwhile concept, and you've put the work in for sure, but it needs a stronger hook, and more trimming of the fat (pun intended).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was okay up until the mtf log, where it ground to a screeching halt and became an unnecessary slog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of this but I have one quibble: if this thing only eats mammals why is it dispensing chicken meat?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The most jarring thing IMO was the one black and white image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The logs killed it for me — the drone log would've been fine but the MTF log after it didn't do much. You had a really nice buildup and low-level story but the MTF log tried to play itself up but got stunted by awkward dialogue and its own length, and if the image at the end was supposed to be a stinger, it didn't work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hungry borger is funny 😂

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You slap this bad boy in it.

[[include :scp-wiki:component:black-highlighter-theme-dev]]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh neat! What triggers it to load instead of the standard one? Do you have to create the page in a certain way, or is it somehow in the body of the text?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a new-ish CSS made by Woedenaz. It's what allows for the captions in the images.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the page screwing with the overall site formatting for anyone else? It's like I'm on a different version of the page, fonts and graphics are slightly different.

item.title: Re: Here In Mother Russia, Burger Eat You!
item.content:encoded:
Image Creds:

Restaurant: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/5d/BurgerKingOrihuelaCosta.jpg

Machine: https://pixabay.com/photos/lost-places-keller-weird-abandoned-2218121/

Kitchen: https://www.flickr.com/photos/vancouver-archives/5858140068

Tunnel: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:14-46-35-f-mutzig.jpg

Closer: Modification of https://www.flickr.com/photos/jjbers/25454577248

item.title: Here In Mother Russia, Burger Eat You!
item.content:encoded:
Why are we still here? Just to suffer? Every night, I can hear it eating. The Whoppers. The nuggets. even The fries. The meat I've lost. The comrades I've lost. It's like they're all being cooked. You can hear it too, can't you?

What if you were the burger? Another little SCP, number buddying with the rest. Thanks to RadioactiveRADS, FloppyPhoenix, Rura, Henzoid, naepicfael, and 17 Incorporeal Brooms for critting.

Catch ya in the deep fryer.
